info.title: Omnidapter Server
info.description: Self-hosted REST API wrapping the Omnidapter calendar integration library

post.description: Create a new connection and begin the OAuth flow.

parameters.name: start

schema.description: Filter events starting after this time (ISO 8601, e.g., 2026-04-06T10:00:00Z)
schema.title: Start

parameters.description: Filter events starting after this time (ISO 8601, e.g., 2026-04-06T10:00:00Z)

parameters.name: end

schema.description: Filter events ending before this time (ISO 8601, e.g., 2026-04-06T18:00:00Z)
schema.title: End

parameters.description: Filter events ending before this time (ISO 8601, e.g., 2026-04-06T18:00:00Z)

parameters.name: limit

schema.description: Maximum number of events to return per page
schema.title: Limit

parameters.description: Maximum number of events to return per page

parameters.name: offset

schema.description: Number of events to skip before returning results
schema.title: Offset

parameters.description: Number of events to skip before returning results

post.description:
Create a short-lived link token for the Connect UI.

Pass the returned token to the embedded or redirect Connect UI as a
Bearer token. The token grants permission to list providers and initiate
a connection, optionally scoped to ``end_user_id``.

Set ``connection_id`` to create a *reconnect* token — the connect UI will
skip provider selection and go directly to the authorization flow for the
existing connection.

AvailabilityResponse.title: AvailabilityResponse
AvailabilityResponse.description: Result of a free/busy availability query.

Booking.title: Booking
Booking.description:
A confirmed or pending booking appointment.

``management_urls`` carries provider-specific action URLs.
Well-known keys: ``"cancel"``, ``"reschedule"``, ``"manage"``, ``"confirm"``.

BookingCustomer.title: BookingCustomer
BookingCustomer.description:
A customer associated with a booking.

``id`` is None when creating a booking without a pre-existing customer record.
Set ``id`` on ``CreateBookingRequest.customer`` to bypass find-or-create.

BookingLocation.title: BookingLocation
BookingLocation.description: A physical or virtual location where bookings can occur.

ConferenceData.title: ConferenceData
ConferenceData.description: Conference/video call information attached to an event.

ConferenceEntryPoint.title: ConferenceEntryPoint
ConferenceEntryPoint.description: A single entry point for a conference (e.g., video URL, phone dial-in).

ConnectSessionRequest.title: ConnectSessionRequest
ConnectSessionRequest.description: Bootstrap token submitted in the request body — never in the URL.

ConnectionConfigField.title: ConnectionConfigField
ConnectionConfigField.description: Describes a required/optional field for provider-specific connection config.

CreateBookingRequest.title: CreateBookingRequest
CreateBookingRequest.description:
Request to create a new booking.

Set ``customer.id`` to bypass find-or-create when the caller already holds
a customer ID from a previous lookup.

CreateCalendarRequest.title: CreateCalendarRequest
CreateCalendarRequest.description: Request model for creating a calendar.

CreateEventRequest.title: CreateEventRequest
CreateEventRequest.description:
Request model for creating a calendar event.

When used via the REST API, ``calendar_id`` is injected from the URL path
parameter and must not be set in the request body.  When using the Python
library directly, callers must supply a non-empty ``calendar_id``.

OAuthScopeGroup.title: OAuthScopeGroup
OAuthScopeGroup.description: A named group of OAuth scopes.

Recurrence.title: Recurrence
Recurrence.description:
Recurrence information for recurring events.

`rules` contains RRULE/EXRULE/RDATE/EXDATE strings (RFC 5545 format).

ReminderOverride.title: ReminderOverride
ReminderOverride.description: A single reminder override for an event.

RescheduleBookingRequest.title: RescheduleBookingRequest
RescheduleBookingRequest.description: Request to reschedule a booking to a new start time.

ServiceType.title: ServiceType
ServiceType.description: A bookable service offered by the provider.

UpdateBookingRequest.title: UpdateBookingRequest
UpdateBookingRequest.description: Request to update an existing booking (partial update — None fields are unchanged).

UpdateCalendarRequest.title: UpdateCalendarRequest
UpdateCalendarRequest.description:
Request model for updating a calendar.

When used via the REST API, ``calendar_id`` is injected from the URL path
parameter.  When using the Python library directly, callers must supply a
non-empty ``calendar_id``.

UpdateEventRequest.title: UpdateEventRequest
UpdateEventRequest.description:
Request model for updating a calendar event.

When used via the REST API, ``calendar_id`` and ``event_id`` are injected
from URL path parameters.  When using the Python library directly, callers
must supply non-empty values for both fields.

LinkTokenAuth.description: Connect UI session token (cs_*). The one-time lt_* bootstrap token is only accepted by POST /connect/init.
